what_it_does: This file is a structured design-system extraction of a 15-slide investor/sales pitch deck for ConceptV. It records the deck's narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, visual-design findings (palette, geometry, typography, spacing, connectors, atoms, zones, chrome modes, sequences, textures), reusable components, and content-length samples.
observations: The file treats a pitch deck as a code/design substrate with extractable, generator-ready rules.
It provides a slide-by-slide attention-flow description for all 15 slides.
It defines a strict visual grammar built around a gold/khaki accent, warm-cream card fills, diagonal hatch texture, a V-stamp footer, a hexagon reserved for the hub, and filled-triangle bullets.
Findings are categorized and each includes a recipe, evidence slide references, an emergent flag, and a generator rule.
It distinguishes two chrome modes: full-bleed photo cover versus white-ground data slides.
Template reuse across slide pairs is interpreted as an intentional rhetorical signal rather than redundancy.
It catalogs components such as StatCard, PillTabRow, NetworkGraphDiagram, BeforeAfterLayout, HatchFooterBand, MarketVerticalSlide, RevenueTree, and PersonCard.
Content samples specify character, word, and line budgets for common slide text slots.
standouts: The hexagon is reserved exclusively for the Virtual Hub platform node; circles represent peer entities.
Gold is restricted to numbers, sub-headings, active states, and bullets; titles are always black.
Template repetition across p-05/p-06, p-10/p-11, and p-14/p-15 is framed as a deliberate scalability argument.
The diagonal hatch texture is treated as a domain signal referencing architectural drafting.
The narrative arc is described as Feel → Problem → Solution → Trust → Path → Economics.
conventions: Follows a consistent JSON schema with category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, and generator_rule for each finding.
References evidence by slide ID (p-01 through p-15).
Labels patterns as emergent or non-emergent.
Components include both a description and a reuse_note.
Content samples include chars, words, and lines.
Uses neutral, descriptive language without ranking or evaluation.
concerns: Some generator rules are absolute ('never', 'always') and may be brittle if the source deck or future variants deviate.
Color and spacing values appear approximate and are not tied to documented source dimensions.
The file does not include the actual slide images, so evidence claims cannot be verified from the file alone.
A few descriptions blend observation with interpretation (e.g., 'deliberate rhetorical arc').
notes: A dense, rule-based design autopsy of a 15-slide ConceptV pitch deck, encoding its visual grammar, narrative flow, and reusable components as generator-ready constraints.
questions: Are the color and spacing values measured from source images or approximated?
What slide dimensions or resolution were used to derive pixel-based measurements?
How was the attention-flow sequence validated—through eyetracking, expert review, or author inference?
Are the listed components intended to map directly to code components, or are they conceptual design atoms?
How do the rules here relate to the mentioned 1-page and 30-page versions?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches; the file extracts a design system from a 15-page pitch deck and additionally encodes generative rules and narrative interpretation.
apparent_themes: Visual grammar as a communicative argument.
Template repetition and structural sameness as scalability rhetoric.
Chromatic restraint with a single gold accent on neutral white and cream.
Material references to architecture and drafting through hatch texture.
Content discipline enforced by geometry and line budgets.
Before/after transformation as the core narrative structure.
Consistent brand signature across every slide.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to convert a static pitch deck into a reproducible design system and content template, capturing not only what the deck looks like but how it argues visually.
novelty: It treats slide-template repetition as an intentional rhetorical device, assigns semantic meaning to shapes (hexagon = hub, circle = peer, triangle = forward motion), ties content budgets to layout geometry, and uses architectural drafting texture as a domain-coded signal.
contribution: A comprehensive set of design tokens and layout rules for a gold-and-cream pitch-deck visual language; a catalog of reusable slide components with content budgets; a method for describing attention flow and narrative arc as structured data; a vocabulary for shape semantics; and content-length samples that can constrain generated copy.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a structured extraction of the ConceptV 15-slide pitch deck's design system, documenting its gold-and-cream visual grammar, per-slide attention flow, reusable components, content budgets, and narrative arc as generative rules.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to turn a specific pitch deck into a reproducible, rule-based design substrate for generating consistent presentations.
